catalogue.definition: Identification of the receiving centre.
catalogue.remark:
Translation:
    See Annex 1 Centre ID definition
Note:
    The Destination ID is irrelevant in CAT150 messages since the flight plan messages are sent to all 10 centres.
    Hence, the centre identifier is set to broadcast.

    The workstation identifier can be ignored.
catalogue.title: Destination ID

catalogue.definition: Identification of the sending centre.
catalogue.remark:
Translation:
    See Destination ID.
Note:
    The Source ID centre identifier will define the flight plan source centre.

    The workstation identifier can be ignored.
catalogue.title: Source ID

catalogue.definition: The event that triggered the message transmission.

values: Flight is beyond extraction area boundary

catalogue.title: Message Type

catalogue.definition: Identification of the flight plan.
catalogue.remark:
Note:
    See Plan and Track Numbers.

    The currently defined range for plan reference numbers used in MADAP is 0 .. 1999. Client systems
    should allow for a range of 0 .. 2047.
catalogue.title: Plan Reference Number

catalogue.definition: Flight identity.
catalogue.title: Callsign

catalogue.definition: Actual transponder code mode 3A of the flight.
catalogue.remark:
Translation:
    Octal representation.

    - zzzz: no code availlable/assigned
    - dd00: code family
    - other: discrete code

    where:

    - z :== 'z'
    - 0 :== '0'
    - d :== '0' .. '7'
catalogue.title: Present Mode 3A

catalogue.definition: Next transponder code mode 3A of the flight.
catalogue.remark:
Translation:
    See Present Mode 3A.
catalogue.title: Next Mode 3A

catalogue.definition: Identification of the flight’s departure aerodrome.
catalogue.remark:
Translation:
    - zzzz: no standard ICAO location identifier
    - other: unique ICAO location identifier

    where:

    - z :== 'z'
catalogue.title: Departure Aerodrome

catalogue.definition: Identification of the flight’s destination aerodrome.
catalogue.remark:
Translation:
    See Departure Aerodrome.
catalogue.title: Destination Aerodrome

catalogue.definition: Type of flight and type of flight plan.
catalogue.title: Type Flags

catalogue.definition: Status of the flight.
catalogue.remark:
Note:
    If an aircraft is set in hold status then the ETO values are increased with 3 hours. 

    RVQ is set for:

    - GAT or GAT SPN: never
    - GAT or OAT CPL: if “W” filed in field 10a [Radio Communication, Navigation and Approach Aid Equipment] of the flightplan

    RVC is set for:

    - GAT or OAT SPN: on controller input or reception of ACT with “RVSM Capable”
    - GAT or OAT CPL (in decreasing priority):
        - Controller input or reception of ABI or ACT with “RVSM Capable”
        - “W” filed in field 10a [Radio Communication, Navigation and Approach Aid Equipment] and “1” filed in field 9a [number of aircraft] in the flightplan.

    RVX is set for:

    - GAT SPN: on controller input “RVSM Exempted”
    - OAT SPN: by default or on controller input “RVSM Exempted”
    - GAT or OAT CPL: if “O”, “M” or “A” filed in field 8b [type of flight] of the flightplan
catalogue.title: Status Flags

catalogue.definition: Flight formation details.
catalogue.title: Aircraft Type

catalogue.definition: Cleared flight level from the sector that has the aircraft under control.
catalogue.remark:
Translation:
    Cleared Flight Level is listed in FLs (100ft).
Note:
    The Cleared Flight Level corresponds to the “current Planned Flight Level”, valid for the sector which
    is currently in communications with the aircraft.
    
    Intermediate flight levels, temporarily assigned by controllers, are not distributed by MADAP.
catalogue.title: Cleared Flight Level

catalogue.definition: Route point descriptions.
catalogue.remark:
Translation:
    See layout table in specification document.
Note:
    For all route point items (140..180), present in a single message the count values are equal. Co4 ordinate (1), description (1), etc. form a single route point.
    
    The maximum number of route points is currently set to 28.

values: B, point with bearing and distance

catalogue.title: Route Points, Description

catalogue.definition: Route point co-ordinates.
catalogue.remark:
Translation:
    Co-ordinate values are in [NM] as Cartesian offsets from 51°00’00”N, 008°00’00”E.
catalogue.title: Route Points, Coordinates

catalogue.definition: Route point position in Lat. / Long. (WSG84).
catalogue.remark:
Note:
    This corresponds to an accuracy of at least 2.4 meters.

items.name: LAT
items.title: Latitude in WGS.84 in Two's Complement Form

items.name: LON
items.title: Longitude in WGS.84 in Two's Complement Form

catalogue.title: Route Points, Geographic Position

catalogue.definition: Estimated times over route points.
catalogue.remark:
Translation:
    Times are specified in 24-hour format. I.e. ranging from 00:00 to 23:59.
catalogue.title: Route Points, Time

catalogue.definition: Planned flight level over route point.
catalogue.remark:
Translation:
    The planned flight levels are given in FLs (100 ft).
Note:
    All flight levels have the same value, equal to the “Current Planned Flight Level”. This is the Planned
    Flight Level valid for the sector which is in communication with the aircraft.
catalogue.title: Route Points, Flight Level

catalogue.definition: Requested flight level over route point.
catalogue.remark:
Translation:
    The planned flight levels are given in FLs (100 ft).
catalogue.title: Route Points, Requested Flight Level

catalogue.definition: Filed true air speed over route point.
catalogue.remark:
Translation:
    The true airspeed is indicated in Knots [NM/h].
catalogue.title: Route Points, Speed

catalogue.definition: Current control position in charge of the aircraft.
catalogue.remark:
Translation:
    Single character controller ids are sent with a leading space character. This can be interpreted as a
    right aligned value.
catalogue.title: Controller ID

catalogue.definition:
Field 18 free text information. Contains subfields, each starting with a 3 or 4 letter keyword followed by
forward slash; e.g. RMK/free text.
catalogue.title: Field 18

catalogue.definition: The track number of the track that has been correlated to the flight plan.
catalogue.remark:
Note:
    See Plan and Track Numbers.
catalogue.title: Correlated Track Number

catalogue.definition: Maximum plan count is the maximum number of possible active plans.
catalogue.remark:
Note:
    The maximum number of active plans is fixed and, at present, set to 302.
catalogue.title: Maximum Plan Count

catalogue.definition: Number of plans that were sent during the last update cycle.
catalogue.remark:
Note:
    The number of extracted plans should be equal to the number of plans received between the start of
    cycle and end of cycle messages.
catalogue.title: Number of Plans

catalogue.definition: Array of correlated plan/track combinations valid in the Maastricht UAC Area of Interest.
catalogue.remark:
Note:
    See Plan and Track Numbers.
catalogue.title: Newly Correlated Plans

catalogue.definition: Array of de-correlated plans.
catalogue.remark:
Note:
    Contains an array of Plan Numbers.
    See Plan and Track Numbers.
catalogue.title: Newly De-correlated Plans

catalogue.definition: Array of conflicting track/track combinations.
catalogue.remark:
Note:
    See Plan and Track Numbers.
catalogue.title: Tracks in Conflict

preamble:
The main purpose of I150 messages is to distribute Flight Plan data to clients. However, other data
items can be sent using I150 as well. I150 messages can be divided into the following sub-categories:

- Flight Plan (Short & Complete)
- Start/End of cycle
- Correlation/De-correlation
- Conflict Alert
title: MADAP Plan Server - Flight Data Message
